notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: CreateMediaWorkflow, GetMediaWorkflow, ListMediaWorkflows, ChangeMediaWorkflowCompartment, UpdateMediaWorkflow, and DeleteMediaWorkflow are present in v65.110.0.

Pinned OCI SDK contract: create, get, and update return the MediaWorkflow body directly and do not expose service-local work-request identifiers; delete returns only headers. MediaWorkflow exposes only ACTIVE, NEEDS_ATTENTION, and DELETED lifecycle states.

Pinned provider coverage is unconfirmed. formal/sources.lock still points at github.com/oracle/terraform-provider-oci@eb653febb1bab4cc6650a96d404a8baf36fdf671, but the accessible local provider/docs layout did not expose a checked-in MediaWorkflow resource or data source. The providerResource and datasource names therefore stay provisional OCI-style placeholders, and the checked-in mutability docs fixture is a conservative repo-authored fallback because the live Terraform Registry resource page is JavaScript-only.

Repo-authored runtime keeps ChangeMediaWorkflowCompartment out of scope. The published controller-backed contract treats compartmentId as replacement-only while reconciling only displayName, tasks, mediaWorkflowConfigurationIds, parameters, freeformTags, and definedTags in place.

Repo-authored runtime treats create and update as synchronous read-after-write flows. NEEDS_ATTENTION is terminal failure without requeue, and delete confirmation relies on follow-up GetMediaWorkflow or fallback ListMediaWorkflows rereads until OCI reports DELETED or NotFound.

Matching create-time locks are normalized out of post-create parity checks by ignoring OCI-populated timeCreated metadata. Changing the requested locks after create remains explicit unsupported drift rather than an implicit add/remove lock flow.
